title: wurmbea dioica subsp. dioica
intro: Wurmbea dioica subsp. dioica, commonly known as the flowering onion, is a plant species within the Colchicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its tuberous roots, which contain colchicine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Wurmbea dioica subsp. dioica include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and treat certain gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be approached with caution due to the potential toxicity of its active compounds.
